programmes.show_description: 1978. Thriller. Director: Daryl Duke. Stars: Elliott Gould, Susannah York, Christopher Plummer, John Candy. A bank teller skims $50,000 during a robbery, but the crook wants it back.

programmes.show_name: Who is Killing the Great Chefs of Europe? 1978
programmes.show_description: Comedy. Directed by Ted Kotcheff. Stars George Segal, Jacqueline Bisset & Robert Morley. Mystery abounds when one by one, the greatest chefs in Europe are being killed.
1963. Who Killed Eleanora Davis? Directed by Herman Hoffman. Stars Gene Barry, Gary Conway & Regis Toomey. An exhibit in a traveling carnival is a supposedly genuine electric chair.
1960. Amnesty. Stars Michael Ansara, Robert Warwick & Biff Elliot. The governor declares a general amnesty, which allows Sam's prisoners to be set free.
1949. Thriller. Director: Edward Dmytryk. Stars: Robert Newton, Sally Gray & Phil Brown. A cuckolded psychiatrist kidnaps his wife's lover and hides him away while he plans his revenge.
1991. Family. Director: Nils Gaup. Stars Stian Smestad, Louisa Haigh & Gabriel Byrne. A Norwegian boy in 1850's England goes to work as a cabin boy and discovers some of his shipmates are pirates.
Remember those intriguing white dots in the cinema? Meet Mike Taylor, a lifetime projectionist with some fascinating stories.
1946. Drama. Directed by Lawrence Huntington. Stars Eric Portman, Dulcie Gray, Derek Farr & Roland Culver. The son of a Victorian hangman is driven insane by thoughts of his father's profession...
The Shareout. 1961. Directed by Gerard Glaister. Stars Bernard Lee, Alexander Knox & William Russell. Mike Stafford helps Scotland Yard solve a case that has already caused three deaths.
The Moneylender. 1955. Stars Richard Greene, Victor Woolf & Alfie Bass. Forced to hide in Sherwood Forest, Robin joins Scatlock's band. To prove his worth, he is sent to deal with a moneylender.
1954. Comedy. Directed by Ralph Thomas. Stars Dirk Bogarde, Muriel Pavlow & Kenneth More. The adventures of a new medical student and his friends at St. Swithin's hospital in London.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life Racing to the Start
programmes.show_description: 1967. An exciting look at individuals who have made racing cars, with an insight into the research and technical skill involved.
The Man with the Ticking Head. 1961. Stars John Ireland, June Thorburn & Richard Wordsworth. A jewel robbery turns to a murder case when the suspect is found dead in a hotel room.
1946. War. Director: Herbert Wilcox. Stars Anna Neagle, Michael Wilding, Coral Browne & Edward Rigby. A missing Wren returns from a desert isle and finds her husband has a new wife and son.
Friendly Relations. 1962. Stars Brian Blessed, Joseph Brady & Frank Windsor. PCs Smith and Weir come across two small children locked in a room while their mother and father holiday in Blackpool.
1969. Detective & Mystery Drama. Divide and Conquer. Stars Alfred Burke, Peter Cellier & Pauline Delaney. Old habits die hard when Frank intervenes seeing a crime take place.
Caroline Munro is back this week opening the Cellar Club with the next episode of the 1970s series, Supernatural!
Night of the Marionettes. 1977. Stars Gordon Jackson, Kathleen Byron & Vladek Sheybal. A literary critic visits the place 'Frankenstein' was written but did this book come from fact or fiction?
Caroline Munro is back to introduce the next two treats in store for tonight. First we have Tales of Frankenstein, followed by the 1940s horror film The Devil Bat!
1958. Horror. Director: Curt Siodmak. Stars Anton Diffring, Helen Westcott & Don Megowan. Dr. Frankenstein has finished his creation, but the monster is unresponsive. He must try something new...
1940. Horror. Director: Jean Yarbrough. Stars Bela Lugosi, Suzanne Kaaren & Dave O' Brien. A mad scientist develops an aftershave that causes his bats to kill anyone who wears it.
The night is not over yet, Caroline Munro is here to give us a glimpse into what is coming up next week at the Cellar Club.

programmes.show_name: Hammer House of Mystery and Suspense
programmes.show_description: And the Wall Came Tumbling Down. 1984. Stars Barbi Benton & Gareth Hunt. A deconsecrated church is destroyed, unleashing an evil that has been hidden for over 300 years.
1999. Drama. Directed by Deric Longden. Stars Pete Postlethwaite & Thora Hird. When Deric's eccentric mother suffers from a stroke, leaving her lost for words, their world undergoes a huge change.
1957. Drama. Directed by Ken Hughes. Stars Victor Mature, Diana Dors & Patrick Allen. A former US serviceman moves to England after WWII but comes caught up in a dangerous smuggling ring.

programmes.show_name: A Lifetime Ago with The Baim Collection
programmes.show_description: Enchanted Cities. 1957. Narrated by Kenneth Macleod. A tour of the beautiful Italian cities of Florence and Venice.
Hacienda/Bullfight. 1986. Leaving behind a cold and wet England, Jack Hargreaves jets off to Nueva Andalucía where 13-year old Jimmy is learning horsemanship the Spanish way.
1913. Sci-Fi. Director: J. Wallett Waller. The first silent British sci-fi feature (with new score). A Martian visits earth to cure a selfish man (similar to 'A Christmas Carol') on Christmas Eve.

programmes.show_name: Food Flash Chester Cod Liver Oil
programmes.show_description: 1945. A short video from the Cheerful Charlie Chester Show in 1945. This describes the health benefits of cod liver oil for children.
Captured. 1975. Stars Simon Cuff, Mandy Jenner & Jerold Wells. Sir Richard and Peterkin pursue a priest who has made off with a priceless relic! Black Arrow returns to call out murderous outlaws.
1997. Stars Brendan Pollecutt, Fezile Mpela & Robert Finlayson. Dean defeats the Lord but can only bathe in the glory for a brief moment as the challenge has only just begun.
The Lesser Breed. 1957. Stars William Russell, Cyril Smith & Ronald Leigh-Hunt. A village is being terrorised; King Arthur sends Sir Lancelot who discovers the Vikings are doing the terrorizing.
Ring Around the Riddler. 1967. Stars Adam West, Burt Ward, Yvonne Craig & Joan Collins. The Riddler challenges Batman to a fight in a boxing champion. The Riddler has no plans to play fair.
Dead End Trail. 1955. Stars Roy Rogers, Trigger and Dale Evans. A young man, unable to put up with his father, sees a reward poster for a gang leader. He sets out to find them and join the gang.
Avalanche. 1967. Stars Francis Matthews, Cy Grant & Jeremy Wilkin. When the Frost Line Outer Space Defence System is threatened, Captain Scarlet and Lieutenant Green are sent to investigate.
The Trap. 1959. Stars: Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. By using a traitor planted in Tell's camp, Gessler plans to smash the resistance movement in one swoop.
Gentleman Jack and the Lady. 1957. Stars Robert Shaw, Hazel Court & Peter Hammond. Tempest clashes with a French privateer as they scheme against each other to win a prize.
The Knight Who Came To Dinner. 1956. Stars Richard Greene, Victor Woolf & Archie Duncan. Little John and Friar Tuck capture a knight, but are concerned about his value judging by his equipment.
1954. Sci-fi Mystery. Directed by W. Lee Wilder. Stars Peter Graves, James Seay & Steve Pendleton. An atomic scientist injured in a plane crash makes a claim that he was abducted by aliens.
1953. The Candlelight Murder. Directed by Ken Hughes. Stars Jack Lambert, Gerald Case, Robert Cawdron & Clifford Buckton. An inspector unmasks the killer of an elderly hermit. (S1, E03)
1959. Action. Director: Paul Wendkos. Stars Cliff Robertson, Gia Scala & Teru Shimada. In 1942, submarine commander Jeff Conway secretly photographs Japanese aircraft carriers in the Coral Sea.
Founder of Talking Pictures TV and one half of the Footage Detectives, Noel Cronin, shares shows and films not to be missed throughout the end of March and the beginning of April.
Divide and Conquer. 1961. Stars Peter Graves, Anthony Wickert & Harry Dearth. While on a mapping expedition, the members are captured by local bush rangers who hatch a plan to ransom them.
1978. Adventure. Director: Tony Maylam. Stars Michael York, Jenny Agutter & Simon MacCorkindale. Set in 1901, a pair of British yachtsmen discover a German plot to invade England.
1956. Directed by Eric Marquis. From Kilkeel in Northern Ireland to Lincoln Cathedral, travel across the four corners of the United Kingdom in 1956.
Poor Cecile. 1963. Stars Rupert Davies, Joan Sanderson & Anthony Jacobs. Old Cecile has a crush on Maigret; he doesn't believe her story about a midnight intruder, until she's murdered.
Honour and Obey. 1976. Stars Gemma Jones, Donald Burton & Bryan Coleman. Hardly flattered, Louisa has caught the eye of the Prince of Wales and is pressed into marrying Augustus Trotter.
1987. Thriller. Director: Peter Yates. Stars Cher, Dennis Quaid & Liam Neeson. A homeless man is accused of murder and a public defender is tasked with mounting his legal defence.
1976. Comedy. Stars Barry Evans, Judy Geeson & Diana Dors. Joe loves picking up dolly birds so his job as a cabbie is perfect! Forget 'south of the river', these beauties are going all the way!
